summary: The Reverser tool now has a native pe-constructor-installs analyzer for allocator -> constructor -> owner-slot install patterns, and it successfully rediscovered the previously hand-mapped auth-controller installation at client+0x198D0.

tool_capability.role: Scan one or more PE function ranges for direct allocator calls, recover allocation size and constructor arguments, then identify stores of the constructor return value into object/client slots.
implemented_behavior: Takes an explicit allocator target and optional constructor and slot-offset filters.
Recovers allocator size and secondary argument from nearby RCX/RDX immediate setup.
Requires the constructor RCX argument to be sourced from the allocator return in RAX.
Tracks constructor-return may-aliases through short branch-merge windows so null-allocation fallback blocks do not hide the successful install path.
Recognizes both direct base+displacement stores and LEA-derived slot-pointer stores such as LEA RBX, [RDI+0x198D0] followed by MOV [RBX], RCX.

relationship_to_prior_findings: Confirms the manual auth-controller constructor-chain milestone without changing its conclusions.
Makes the allocation size, constructor callsite, constructor arguments, and client slot install reproducible with a single Reverser command.
The recognizer is now reusable for adjacent bootstrap/controller slots such as client+0x19830 and client+0x19838, plus future object-lifetime mapping work.
limitations: The analyzer is intentionally scoped to direct x64 call patterns and local decoded ranges.
The post-constructor return tracking is a may-alias pass, so it should be used with constructor and slot filters for high-confidence triage.
It does not yet build a full control-flow graph or prove destructor ownership; those remain follow-up analyzer candidates.
next_targets: Run pe-constructor-installs over neighboring bootstrap slots to map client+0x19830 and client+0x19838.
Use the new recognizer on login/session and worker scheduler constructor neighborhoods to build object-lifetime maps faster.
Extend the analyzer later with destructor/replace helper recognition once more install patterns are collected.
